STORY OF RAILROADS.


GREAT FIGHT

pages.raw_text:
the Milwaukee was when he learn
ed of it's efforts to gain control OF
the Inland Empire system OF cleo
trio railways, a Spokane enterprise
with 200 miles of city lines in Spo-
kane and interurban lines over
northern Idaho and eastern Wash
ington, in which Hill himself held
large minority interest The
Milwaukee wanted the Inland lines
for an entrance into Spokane. Hill
bought the control of the Inland
system for nearly 100000 in cold
cash and headed off the Milwaukee,


The defeat of the Milwaukee in
the control of the Inland was of
but temporary advantage. The
Rockefeller agents next walked
into Spokane and began buying up
business blocks for downtown
terminals, and paid prices for
ground that set a new high water
mark in real estate values. This
week comes the announcement
that the Milwaukee will build q
loop from Plummer, Idaho, into
Spokane, a total distance Of some
20 miles, which will enable it to
run its through trains through this
city one block closer to the heart
of the business section than Hills
